---
title: "About Me"
subtitle: "*A little more about me, both in and out of work*"
lightbox: true
match: false
---
# EDUCATION AND RESEARCH
I am currently an undergraduate pre-law student in my second year at UC Santa Barbara, majoring in [Environmental Studies](https://es.ucsb.edu/){target="_blank"}, [Geography](https://www.geog.ucsb.edu/){target="_blank"}, and [Philosophy](https://www.philosophy.ucsb.edu/){target="_blank"}, with a minor in [Legal Humanities](https://www.duels.ucsb.edu/minors/legal-humanities-minor){target="_blank"}. Within my fields of study, I have a particular interest in environmental policy, and love exploring how ecological research and innovations intersect with legislation to create feasible and sustainable environmental solutions. In particular, I hope to further explore the ecological processes behind larger ecosystemic regulation, and use those explorations to facilitate actionable legislative decisions pertaining to endangered species policy.

## WORK
At the moment, I am seeking employment opportunities in conservation research and environmental policy. Since 2025, I have worked with [Ken Hiltner](https://english.ucsb.edu/people/faculty/hiltner-ken/){target="_blank"} at UCSB as an Undergraduate Research Assistant, using ecocritical perspectives and methods to approach historical and modern media, then exploring the impacts this media has had on our modern environmental perceptions. Outside of UCSB, I have been conducting personal research about the ecological impacts of invasive shrew-species on island locales since 2023, specifically focusing on the Irish Isles and the effects nonnative greater-white-toothed shrews have had on the local ecology. 

I'm always looking forward for the opportunity to collaborate on new/existing projects with others. Please feel free reach out if you are interested in connecting, working together, or just to say hi! 


---
# INTERESTS AND HOBBIES
<p style ="font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;" p class = "title"> “Nature's creative power is far beyond man's instinct of destruction.” </p> <p style = "font-size: 14px;"> \- Jules Verne, *Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea* </p>

Outside of school and work, I love listening to music, playing games, and exploring the outdoors. As an ES major, I am constantly exposed to the fact that our anthropogenic actions have created a persistent underlying threat to our environments. Subsequently, I think it is extremely important -- now more than ever -- for people to go outside, appreciate our natural landscapes, and see what it is that we need to preserve, protect, and restore. California is a locale that is especially great for this, bolstering with some of the richest diversities of nature, something I am constantly grateful for! Some of my favorite outdoor activities include climbing, hiking, and fishing. 

## CLIMBING
I started climbing when I came to Santa Barbara. It's quickly become one of my favorite sports, in part due to the problem-solving physical aspect of it, and in part due to the wonderful community that I've found. Within UCSB, I am an officer on the [Climbing Team](https://recreation.ucsb.edu/sport-club/coed-climbing){target="_blank"}, as well as an employee at the ACC -- or [Adventure Climbing Center](https://recreation.ucsb.edu/adventure-programs/adventure-climbing-center){target="_blank"} -- the on-campus recreational climbing center. Personally, I think that climbing is one of the best ways to both meet new people, and to spend some time outdoors!

::: {layout-ncol=3}
![](media/bree-climbing.JPG){group="jtree" width="300"}

![](media/satellite-freesolo.JPG){group="jtree" width="300"}

![](media/filip-climbing.JPG){group="jtree" width="300"}
:::

Here are some pictures from my recent trip to Joshua Tree. If you're interested, you can see more about this trip on the UCSB climbing team instagram [{{< fa camera title= "camera" >}}](https://www.instagram.com/ucsbclimbing/){target="_blank"} (which I run!)

## HIKING
Climbing outdoors is great. But sometimes it's nice to just enjoy the nature without having to throw yourself at a wall. I also love hiking with friends, and exploring beyond the crag!

[hiking map]

```{r}

#| label: reading-packages

# importing packages
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(leaflet)
library(leaflet.extras) 
library(sf)

```

```{r}

#| label: cleaning-sp

# creating object: santa_paula
santa_paula <- "Punchbowls_via_Santa_Paula_and_Last_Chance_Trail.gpx"
# creating layers
st_layers(santa_paula)
# creating object: sp_track
sp_track <- read_sf(santa_paula, 
                    # use track_points data
                    layer = "track_points") |> 
  # separate time column
  separate(col = time, 
           # date category
            into = c("date",
                     # time point category
                     "timepoint"), 
            sep = " ", 
            remove = FALSE)

#| label: cleaning-db
# creating object: devils_bridge
devils_bridge <- "Devils_Bridge_via_Mescal_Trailhead.gpx"
# creating layers
st_layers(devils_bridge)
# creating object: db_track
db_track <- read_sf(devils_bridge, 
                    # use track_points data
                    layer = "track_points") |> 
  # separate time column
  separate(col = time, 
           # date category
            into = c("date",
                     # time point category
                     "timepoint"), 
            sep = " ", 
            remove = FALSE)

#| label: cleaning-hs
# creating object: devils_bridge
hotsprings <- "Hot_Springs_Trail_and_Edison_Catway.gpx"
# creating layers
st_layers(hotsprings)
# creating object: db_track
hs_track <- read_sf(hotsprings, 
                    # use track_points data
                    layer = "track_points") |> 
  # separate time column
  separate(col = time, 
           # date category
            into = c("date",
                     # time point category
                     "timepoint"), 
            sep = " ", 
            remove = FALSE)

#| label: cleaning-ac
# creating object: antelope_canyon
antelope_canyon <- "Upper_Antelope_Canyon_Trail.gpx"
# creating layers
st_layers(antelope_canyon)
# creating object: db_track
ac_track <- read_sf(antelope_canyon, 
                    # use track_points data
                    layer = "track_points") |> 
  # separate time column
  separate(col = time, 
           # date category
            into = c("date",
                     # time point category
                     "timepoint"), 
            sep = " ", 
            remove = FALSE)

trail_points <- data.frame(
  lat = c(34.462712, 34.8977, 34.4547, 36.8625),
  lng = c(-119.640097, -111.8077, -119.0557, -111.3747),
  label = c("Santa Paula Punchbowls", "Devils Bridge", "Hot Springs", "Antelope Canyon")
)

```


```{r}

#| label: ggplots

# ggplot: sp_track data
ggplot(sp_track) +
  # layer one: sf
  geom_sf() +
  # changing theme
  theme_bw()

# ggplot: db_track data
ggplot(db_track) +
  # layer one: sf
  geom_sf() +
  # changing theme
  theme_bw()

# ggplot: hs_track data
ggplot(hs_track) +
  # layer one: sf
  geom_sf() +
  # changing theme
  theme_bw()

# ggplot: ac_track data
ggplot(ac_track) +
  # layer one: sf
  geom_sf() +
  # changing theme
  theme_bw()

```

```{r}

#| label: base-map

# create trail map
trail_map <- leaflet(trail_points) |> 
  # use ESRI background
  addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.WorldImagery) |> 
  addMarkers(lng = ~lng,
             lat = ~lat,
             popup = ~label,
             clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions()
) |> 
  # adding mini map
  addMiniMap(toggleDisplay = TRUE, minimized = TRUE) |> 
  setView(lng =  -117.5, lat = 38.5, zoom = 5) |> 
    # reset map button
  leaflet.extras::addResetMapButton()

# printing map
trail_map

```


```{r}

#| label: creating-trails

sp_trail <- sp_track |> 
  st_combine() |> 
  st_cast(to = "LINESTRING") |> 
  st_sf()

db_trail <- db_track |> 
  st_combine() |> 
  st_cast(to = "LINESTRING") |> 
  st_sf()

hs_trail <- hs_track |> 
  st_combine() |> 
  st_cast(to = "LINESTRING") |> 
  st_sf()

ac_trail <- ac_track |> 
  st_combine() |> 
  st_cast(to = "LINESTRING") |> 
  st_sf()

```


```{r}

#| label: adding-hikes

trail_map |> 
  addPolylines(data = sp_trail,
               color = "chartreuse",
               weight = 2,
               opacity = 1.0) |> 
  addPolylines(data = db_trail,
               color = "chartreuse",
               weight = 2,
               opacity = 1.0) |> 
  addPolylines(data = hs_trail,
               color = "chartreuse",
               weight = 2,
               opacity = 1.0) |> 
  addPolylines(data = ac_trail,
               color = "chartreuse",
               weight = 2,
               opacity = 1.0)

```

```{r}
# saving the prepared leaflet plot as a html file
htmltools::save_html(trail_map, "trail.html")
```


Here are some of my favorite and recent hikes that I've done.

## FISHING
When I was younger, my parents would often take my brother and I fishing. My father is from Qingdao, and grew up fishing and swimming by the beach, an experience that he wanted us to have as well. Unfortunately, each of us only picked up one half of that -- my brother is great at fishing but can't really swim while I did swim team and can't really fish. But despite the fact that I'm *horrible* at fishing, I still love going out to the water to do some angling -- hopefully I'll get better soon!

[fishing minigame - catch fish and tell info abt it -- all of the fish will be ones that I have caught before]
